name: Tabs
description: Horizontal tab row with a single 2px (`sys.borderWidth.thin`) `sys.color.onSurface` indicator that slides between the active tab's bottom edge on selection. Default content-section switcher.

onChange.description: Fired with the next value when a tab is selected.

embedded.description: Composition mode flag. When `true` (or when Tabs is a direct child of `.chorus-section` / `.chorus-feed`), enters **embedded mode**: zeroes its own `background` + `padding` so chrome defers to the host. Pass explicitly inside `<Section>` / `<Feed>` for the contract to be visible in JSX; the DOM-ancestry safety net in styles.css also activates the mode when omitted. See `compositionModes` in `tabs.family.json`.

adaptiveWidth.description: Row measures Σ(tab.intrinsicWidth) vs container.clientWidth on every layout pass. Fits → tabs stretch (flex: 1 1 0); exceeds → tabs hold content width (flex: 0 0 auto) and the row scrolls horizontally with a trailing Edge fade. Re-evaluated via ResizeObserver on container + tab row. Not a caller-supplied prop.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the tab is in.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: The Tabs row is a horizontal scroller (`overflow-x: auto`, which coerces `overflow-y: auto`), so an outward ring would clip at the row's top/bottom edges. Segmented and Rounded re-anchor the chip's ring the same way — their rows are scrollers too.

ring.outerLayerPosition: depth 0..2px from the tab edge (the outer stroke)
ring.insetLayerPosition: depth 2..3px from the tab edge (the counter-ring just inside the outer stroke)
ring.implementation: inset box-shadow on the tab's `::after` overlay (sits above the state-overlay `::before`, the label/icon, and the underline indicator). Constrained strictly inside the tab's footprint and never exceeds it.

focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

forbidden: active indicator painted with sys.color.brand — underline uses sys.color.primary for the indicator
tabs wrapped in extra horizontal padding — tabs is full-bleed by family declaration
tab label sizing below sys.typo.label.md (14px) — smaller text breaks Korean / CJK hierarchy
manual underline drawn via `border-bottom:` — indicator is the `::after` overlay, not a real border
